data.title: Ehime Bank Overhauls Executive Lineup Ahead of June Shareholders’ Meeting
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ehime Bank, Ltd. ( (JP:8541) ) has provided an announcement.

Ehime Bank has announced a broad reshuffle of its leadership, with the board tentatively approving new appointments to director, corporate auditor, and executive officer roles, to be formally decided at the June 26, 2026 shareholders’ meeting. Managing Executive Officer Tetsuya Nakamura is set to become a managing director, while outside director Fumiaki Matsubara and standing corporate auditor Tetsuya Sasaki will join the board and audit ranks as part of a refreshed governance lineup.

Several executives, including branch and department general managers Koichi Nishiyama, Yoichiro Koike, Daisuke Takata, and Kentaro Yano, are slated to be promoted to executive officer, signaling a pipeline of internal talent moving into senior management. The changes, which also include the retirement of one outside director and one standing corporate auditor, are expected to strengthen the bank’s oversight and operational structure by aligning leadership roles more closely with core businesses such as human resources, planning, securities, international affairs, and ship finance.

More about Ehime Bank, Ltd.

Ehime Bank, Ltd. is a regional financial institution listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market, focused on commercial banking services in Japan. The bank serves local customers through branch-based lending, deposit-taking, and specialized units such as ship finance and securities and international affairs, positioning itself as a key provider of financial services in the Ehime area and surrounding regions.

Average Trading Volume: 142,328

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen68.65B

data.title: Rox Resources corrects drilling data but affirms high-grade gold outlook
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Rox Resources Limited ( (AU:RXL) ) has shared an update.

Rox Resources has issued a correction to its 21 April 2026 drilling update, clarifying that several high-grade gold intersections were mis-stated in the body of the announcement and that drillhole collar coordinates were originally reported in a mine operational grid rather than in the MGA_GDA94 system. The company stressed that the corrected intervals were already accurate in the supporting data, and that the overall significance of the results remains intact, still underpinning plans to de-risk initial production fronts scheduled for late 2026 and highlighting scope to grow output beyond the initial mine plan.

Revised collar locations and an updated description of the grid system have been provided to ensure alignment with JORC reporting standards, while confirming that no other elements of the technical disclosure have changed. For investors and stakeholders, the clarification is positioned as a technical clean-up rather than a downgrade of the project’s exploration outcomes, preserving confidence in the quality and potential of the delineated high-grade ounces.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:RXL) stock is a Buy with a A$1.15 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Rox Resources Limited stock, see the AU:RXL Stock Forecast page.

More about Rox Resources Limited

Rox Resources Limited is a West Australian gold exploration and development company focused on advancing its gold projects toward production. Listed on the ASX under the ticker RXL, the company targets high-grade gold mineralisation to support upcoming mining operations and expand its production profile in the region.

Average Trading Volume: 3,151,435

Hingtex Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1968) ) has issued an update.

Hingtex Holdings Ltd. plans to amend and restate its existing memorandum and articles of association to modernize corporate governance and shareholder engagement. The move is aimed at giving the company more flexibility in how it conducts general meetings, including the option to hold them virtually via online meeting technologies.

The proposed changes also seek to align the company’s constitutional documents with Hong Kong laws and the latest listing rules, while making various housekeeping updates. These amendments will be put to shareholders for approval by special resolution at the annual general meeting scheduled for 28 May 2026, with full details to be provided in a forthcoming circular.

More about Hingtex Holdings Ltd.

Hingtex Holdings Ltd., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1968, operates in the textile and garment sector. The company is governed by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical Hong Kong-listed corporate governance structure.

Winsome Resources Limited ( (AU:WR1) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Winsome Resources Limited has updated the market on a change to its capital structure following the expiry of a tranche of listed options. The company reported that 1,000,000 WR1AF options, which were due to expire on 24 April 2026 with an exercise price of $1.30, have lapsed unexercised, resulting in these securities ceasing to exist.

The cessation of these options slightly reduces the pool of potential future equity dilution for existing shareholders, while leaving the current issued share capital unchanged. This routine update reflects ongoing compliance with ASX disclosure rules and provides clarity to investors regarding the company’s outstanding securities profile.

data.title: Tivan Secures Timor-Leste Licenses for High-Grade Copper-Gold Projects
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tivan Limited ( (AU:TVN) ) has provided an update.

Tivan Limited has formally received six Exploration and Evaluation Licenses for the Baucau and Ossu projects in Timor-Leste, covering 289 square kilometres and further consolidating its position in the country’s emerging minerals sector. The licenses were presented at official community ceremonies hosted by Timor-Leste’s Minister of Petroleum and Mineral Resources and attended by national, municipal and community leaders alongside Tivan’s Australian and local teams.

The events were framed as a key step in building Tivan’s social licence to operate, introducing the company to local stakeholders and reinforcing government support for its activities. With earlier sampling at Baucau and Ossu indicating high-grade copper-gold and cobalt mineralisation, the reissued licenses following Tivan’s acquisition of the projects from Beacon Minerals are expected to underpin the company’s exploration pipeline and enhance its strategic footprint in the southern Outer Banda Arc region.

More about Tivan Limited

Tivan Limited (ASX: TVN) is an Australian resources company focused on exploration and evaluation of mineral projects, with a growing presence in Timor-Leste. Its portfolio now includes the Turiscai, Baucau and Ossu projects, targeting high-grade copper-gold and associated minerals in underexplored but prospective geological settings along established transport corridors.

data.title: ACC trims substantial stake in NZX Limited
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

NZX ( (NZSTF) ) has shared an update.

Accident Compensation Corporation has reduced its substantial shareholding in NZX Limited, selling 3,603,095 ordinary shares on-market between December 2025 and April 2026. Following these transactions, ACC’s holding fell from 22,215,170 to 18,612,075 shares, lowering its stake from 6.762% to 5.653% of NZX’s quoted voting products.

The reduction in ACC’s stake represents a sizeable divestment by a major institutional investor in New Zealand’s stock exchange operator. While ACC remains a substantial product holder, the move may signal portfolio rebalancing or changing investment priorities, and is likely to be closely watched by market participants given NZX’s role at the core of the country’s capital markets.

More about NZX

NZX Limited operates New Zealand’s primary securities exchange, providing listing, trading and market services for equities and other financial products. The company plays a central role in the country’s capital markets infrastructure, facilitating investment, price discovery and liquidity for issuers and investors.

data.description:

An update from Volt Power Group Ltd ( (AU:VPR) ) is now available.

Volt Group Limited has called its 2026 annual general meeting for 28 May 2026 in Kewdale, Western Australia, setting 26 May 2026 as the record date for shareholders eligible to vote. The meeting will consider the company’s financial, directors’ and auditors’ reports for the year ended 31 December 2025, with these materials provided in the latest annual report.

Shareholders will also vote on an advisory resolution to adopt the 2025 remuneration report, which, while non-binding, will guide the board’s future pay policies. The notice outlines voting restrictions for key management personnel and their related parties, reinforcing governance standards around executive remuneration decisions.

More about Volt Power Group Ltd

Volt Group Limited is an Australian company listed under ACN 009 423 189. The company has a shareholder base entitled to vote on corporate matters under the Corporations Act and relevant regulations, and operates under standard Australian corporate governance and reporting requirements.

data.title: Neuren Files Updated ASX Governance Appendix, Confirms Compliance With Key Standards
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Neuren Pharmaceuticals Limited ( (AU:NEU) ) just unveiled an update.

Neuren Pharmaceuticals has lodged its Appendix 4G and confirmed that its corporate governance statement for the year ended 31 December 2025 is included in its latest annual report and current as of 31 March 2026. The filing, authorised by CFO and Company Secretary Lauren Frazer, affirms that the company follows the ASX Corporate Governance Council’s key recommendations, including a formal board charter, director vetting and written agreements for directors and executives.

The disclosures indicate that Neuren’s company secretary reports directly to the board through the chair, reinforcing the board’s oversight of governance processes. By mapping where each governance disclosure can be found, the Appendix 4G serves as both a transparency tool for investors and a compliance check, underscoring Neuren’s adherence to ASX listing rules and established governance standards.
